evals.prompt: I want to run DPO on the HHH preference dataset. Give me a complete script.
evals.expected_output: Runnable DPO script using HHHComparisonBuilder and DPODatasetBuilderFromComparisons
expectations: Uses DPODatasetBuilderFromComparisons from tinker_cookbook.preference.dpo_datasets
Uses HHHComparisonBuilder from tinker_cookbook.recipes.preference.datasets
Sets dpo_beta (should be around 0.1)
Uses a learning rate around 1e-5 (lower than SFT)
Uses train_dpo.Config from tinker_cookbook.preference.train_dpo
The script is complete and runnable
Mentions that DPO works best from an SFT checkpoint, not raw base model
Lists alternative comparison builders (HelpSteer3, UltraFeedback)

evals.prompt: Walk me through setting up a full RLHF pipeline — SFT, reward model, then RL.
evals.expected_output: Three-stage pipeline explanation with checkpoint chaining between stages
expectations: Describes all three stages: SFT, Reward Model training, RL from RM
Shows checkpoint chaining using checkpoint_utils.get_last_checkpoint()
Uses PreferenceModelBuilderFromChatRenderer for the reward model in RL stage
Uses PairwisePreferenceRLDatasetBuilder for the RL environment
Sets RL learning rate much lower than SFT (1e-5 vs 2e-4)
Mentions that RM quality should be validated before running RL
Explains the purpose of each stage (not just the code)
Uses ChatDatasetBuilderFromComparisons for RM training (supervised on comparisons)

evals.prompt: I have my own preference data as pairs of good/bad responses. How do I use it with DPO?
evals.expected_output: Custom ComparisonBuilder implementation returning (chosen, rejected) conversation pairs
expectations: Shows how to create a custom ComparisonBuilder
Returns list of (chosen_messages, rejected_messages) tuples
Each message list uses the standard Message format with role and content
Wraps it with DPODatasetBuilderFromComparisons
Mentions that preference data quality matters more than quantity
